skill.name: risk-management-specialist
skill.description: Medical device risk management guidance for ISO 14971 planning, hazard analysis, risk evaluation, risk controls, and post-production monitoring.
skill.summary: Guide ISO 14971 risk management for medical device teams.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and Ruby backtick execution patterns, but review found only documentation text and Markdown formatting. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, command execution logic, or malicious intent was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Matches Plain Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain ordinary documentation words such as description, design, guides, slides, and endpoints. No cryptographic algorithm implementation, hashing call, cipher configuration, or security-sensitive code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matches are visible in prose and headings, not executable code. Context confirms domain text for medical device risk management.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misclassified As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged backticks are Markdown code fences and inline filenames in a resource list. They do not execute commands and do not accept user-controlled input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are Markdown formatting in SKILL.md. No Ruby code, shell invocation, or command execution function is present.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Helper Script Is Non-Malicious
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for semantic threat review. The script prints a placeholder message and contains no file, network, environment, or shell access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script body only calls print with static text. No hidden behavior or data access was found.

content.value_statement: Medical device teams must document risk decisions across design, software, cybersecurity, and post-market work. This skill structures ISO 14971 analysis, controls, verification, and risk file maintenance.
actual_capabilities: Guides ISO 14971 risk management planning and file structure.
Supports hazard identification for hardware, software, use error, and cybersecurity risks.
Helps estimate severity, probability, residual risk, and risk acceptability.
Maps risk controls to verification, validation, and effectiveness evidence.
Connects risk management with QMS, clinical, and post-market processes.
limitations: Does not replace qualified regulatory, clinical, or quality system judgment.
Does not include validated automation scripts or completed templates.
Cannot verify device-specific risks without accurate product evidence.
Requires current regulatory sources for final submission decisions.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Risk Management Plan
use_cases.description: Define scope, responsibilities, risk criteria, lifecycle activities, and risk file expectations for a device program.

use_cases.title: Review Software Safety Risks
use_cases.description: Connect IEC 62304 software classification, hazardous situations, controls, and verification evidence.

use_cases.title: Update Post-Market Risk Evidence
use_cases.description: Assess complaints, surveillance signals, CAPA records, and clinical updates against existing risk controls.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Risk Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an ISO 14971 risk management plan outline for a Class II medical device. Include scope, roles, criteria, and file contents.

prompt_templates.title: Identify Hazards
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a hazard identification checklist for this device description. Cover hardware, software, cybersecurity, use error, and foreseeable misuse.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Controls
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these hazardous situations and proposed controls. Identify missing verification evidence and residual risk questions.

prompt_templates.title: Audit the Risk File
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this risk management file summary against ISO 14971 lifecycle expectations. Find traceability gaps and post-production update needs.

output_examples.input: Assess risk controls for a connected infusion pump update.
output: Control coverage review for authentication, alarm behavior, software failure modes, and misuse scenarios.
Residual risk questions tied to severity, occurrence, detectability, and clinical benefit.
Verification evidence checklist for cybersecurity testing, software validation, and labeling updates.

output_examples.input: Create a risk management file maintenance checklist.
output: Sections for risk plan review, hazard log updates, control verification, CAPA linkage, and surveillance inputs.
Decision prompts for risk-benefit review and acceptability criteria changes.
Traceability checks across design controls, clinical evaluation, and post-market information.

output_examples.input: Review use-related risks for a home diagnostic device.
output: Use scenario map covering setup, sample handling, result interpretation, cleaning, storage, and disposal.
Potential use errors with related harms, contributing factors, and control options.
Human factors evidence needs for critical tasks and labeling comprehension.

best_practices: Provide the device intended use, users, environment, and risk acceptability criteria.
Link each hazard, hazardous situation, harm, control, and verification item.
Review risk files after design changes, complaints, CAPA, and surveillance signals.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generic hazard lists as complete device-specific analysis.
Do not accept residual risks without documented criteria and benefit rationale.
Do not use the skill as a substitute for formal regulatory review.

faq.question: What standard does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on ISO 14971 risk management for medical devices across the product lifecycle.

faq.question: Can it create a complete risk management file?
faq.answer: It can structure and review file contents, but teams must supply validated device evidence.

faq.question: Does it support software risk work?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes IEC 62304 alignment, software hazards, safety classification, and verification links.

faq.question: Does it cover cybersecurity risks?
faq.answer: Yes. It prompts threat modeling, patient safety impact review, controls, and monitoring evidence.

faq.question: Is this legal or regulatory advice?
faq.answer: No. It is workflow guidance and should be reviewed by qualified regulatory professionals.

faq.question: What inputs improve the output?
faq.answer: Provide intended use, device architecture, users, hazards, controls, complaint data, and acceptance criteria.
